results.title: Once there were bluebirds
results.creator_credit: Dick Van Bentham and FilmFair Communications
results.brief_description: Uses animation and live-action to acquaint children with the beauty of nature and the ecological problems caused by the encroachments of modern society. Suitable for primary school level.

results.title: The World of the future
results.creator_credit: Peter Thurling, Douglas Leiterman, Philip S. Hobel and Hobel-Leiterman Productions
results.brief_description: Looks at the changing lifestyles and subcultures of the future, the changing environment and the effect on people, their values and their ability to cope. Includes footage from George Lucas’ first science fiction feature “THX-1138”.

results.title: From Homo Erectus to neanderthal
results.brief_description: Adapted from an MGM film “The Man hunters”, this film discusses the neanderthal man and Homo Erectus and shows how man’s development has been gradual, covering many evolutionary stages. Suitable for Upper Secondary levels.

results.title: Breaking out of The Doll's House
results.creator_credit: Joseph Losey and Learning Corporation of America
results.brief_description: This film is edited from the feature film “The Doll’s House” which is an adaptation of the play by Ibsen, written in 1879. Jane Fonda plays the role of the main character Nora. A series of events in Nora’s married life forces her to turn back on her family and search for her own identity. This film raises discussion on the issues of feminine identity and is recommended for Years 11-12.

results.title: Helen Keller
results.creator_credit: David L. Wolper and Sterling Educational Films
results.brief_description: The story of Helen Keller is the story of a normal child who, at the age of 18 months, was suddenly shut off from the world but, against overwhelming odds, waged a slow, hard but successful battle to re-enter that same world. The child grew into a highly intelligent and sensitive woman who wrote, spoke and labored incessantly for the betterment of others. Helen was born in Tuscumbia, Alabama on June 27, 1880. However, her real life began one day in March of 1887, when she was almost seven years old. She was always to call that the most important day I can remember in my life. It was the day when Annie Sullivan, a 20 year old graduate of the Perkins School for the Blind, came to be her teacher. They were inseparable until Annies death in 1936. This Film depicts events in her life, who, with the aid of her teacher, learned to understand and communicate with others.

results.title: Finders keepers
results.creator_credit: Edmund Penney, Jack Wipper, Lori Productions and Sterling Educational Films
results.brief_description: Illustrates the concept of unfairness in the story of three boys, one of whom has impaired hearing. The boys find a wallet and are unsure what to do when the owner returns. Suitable for primary school level.

results.title: Fair play
results.creator_credit: Edmund Penney, Jack Wipper and Sterling Educational Films
results.brief_description: The film encourages judgements on three situations which occur in play. The experiences of the playground are important social lessons. Sharing, kindness and responsibility for each other’s well being are some of the elements of “fair play”.

results.title: Kittens grow up
results.creator_credit: Hugh Johnston, Suzanne Johnston and McGraw-Hill Films
results.brief_description: Studies the development of kittens from immediately after birth, through learning to walk, until they gain the ability to co-ordinate all their activities. Also covers the role of the mother cat in this development. Sequal to ‘Kittens are born’. Suitable for upper primary level.

results.title: Kittens are born
results.creator_credit: Hugh Johnston, Suzanne Johnston and McGraw-Hill Films
results.brief_description: Follows a female cat from pregnancy through labour and the birth of three kittens. Emphasises the birth process and parental care immediately following birth. Suitable for upper primary level.

results.title: Clay: or the origin of the species
results.creator_credit: Eliot Noyes, Contemporary Films and McGraw-Hill Films
results.brief_description: An art film in which three-dimensional forms of modelling clay are used to express a visual and amusing variation on a theory of evolution as suggested by the simple lifeforms which emerge from graphic motions of a clay sea. Best Film, 6th Annual Annecy film festival.
